column4: Often used as an interjection: kaṣṭam = “damnit!”
Often used at the end of a bahuvrīhiḥ compound to refer to things “beginning with” the prior member(s) of the compound, hence “and so on,” “et cetera.”
The past passive participle of dah “burn.”
This can also be used in more respectful variants by prefixing atra or tatra.
Consult the materials in lesson 13 for the full paradigm of this pronoun.
